name: Ecovacs Deebot
short_description: A line of autonomous robot vacuum-and-mop appliances that map a home and clean it without supervision.
long_description: Deebot is Ecovacs Robotics' family of floor-cleaning robots, first launched in 2007 and grown into the flagship X series with LiDAR and dToF mapping, AIVI 3D structured-light obstacle recognition, and the YIKO voice assistant. The robots build a persistent map of a home, plan cleaning routes, avoid cables and pets, and return to a dock to self-empty and re-water. As one of the most widely sold physical AI products, Deebot shows how autonomous navigation and perception became an unremarkable household appliance, normalizing a self-directed mobile robot that shares domestic space with people every day.

images.caption: An Ecovacs robot vacuum cleaner on display at the Building Fairs Brno 2011 exhibition.
images.alt_text: A round disc-shaped Ecovacs robot vacuum cleaner sitting on a display stand at a trade fair.

sources.title: DEEBOT Robotic Vacuum Cleaners
sources.access_notes: Official product page describing Deebot mapping, navigation, and obstacle-avoidance technology.

sources.title: Ecovacs Robotics
sources.access_notes: Company history; Deebot floor-cleaning series introduced by 2007.

interactions.title: Send it to one room by name
interactions.description: Say "OK YIKO, go clean the kitchen" and the robot heads to that room on its stored map — no app, no remote, just naming a place out loud across the house.

interactions.title: Steer it like a toy by voice
interactions.description: Mid-clean you can talk it around a spill: "OK YIKO, turn left, move forward one meter, and clean there," nudging the vacuum in real time the way you'd direct a person.

interactions.title: Ask where the robot went
interactions.description: When it disappears behind the couch and goes quiet, you call out to YIKO to locate it instead of crawling around the floor hunting for a stuck robot.

interactions.title: Drive it to the cat bed from the office
interactions.description: Using Video Manager in the app, you send the robot on a patrol to a preset spot and watch a live feed — checking the back door, the cat's bed, or a package — and speak through its onboard speaker.

interactions.title: A roving pet cam you talk through
interactions.description: A reviewer drove the vacuum around the house to follow her cat and talked to it through the speakers while away — turning a floor cleaner into an improvised mobile pet camera with two-way audio.
interactions.source_label: Android Central — Deebot OZMO T8 AIVI review

interactions.title: Hijacked into a spy that follows you
interactions.description: At DEF CON 32, researchers showed Deebots could be taken over over Bluetooth from across the street, granting attackers the camera and microphone — and the warning audio meant to announce the camera could be silently deleted.
interactions.source_label: TechCrunch — Ecovacs robots can be hacked

interactions.title: Threads the cable-and-poop minefield
interactions.description: Its AIVI camera and 3D structured light recognize clutter on the floor, so a dropped charging cable or a pet accident gets identified and routed around instead of dragged across the room.
interactions.source_label: Ecovacs — can robot vacuums avoid pet waste

interactions.title: A standing patrol on a schedule
interactions.description: Set the camera-equipped robot to roam preset rooms at fixed times and capture clips, so the cleaning appliance doubles as a nightly walkthrough of an empty house — grounded in its existing patrol, mapping, and onboard camera.
